user_content: I've got a pretty packed schedule with other shows I'm watching, but I think I can fit in "Money Heist" or "Killing Eve" into my routine. I'm also still keeping up with the latest episodes of "The Office" (US) on Hulu, and I'm trying to finish the entire series before the end of February. Do you think it's realistic to finish all of that in time, considering my schedule?

Let's develop quests for an RPG in a fantasy medieval setting. I will give you a couple of NPCs and you propose some quests evolving them for a hero to accomplish.
1) Gregozh, is a horse groom, though a bit old for one. He came to live in the village of Lorelei half a year ago from the the imperial city of Tunz. We don't know much about him.
2) Bendalf, is a village elder and one of the few villagers who can read. He farmed for sixty years and still helps take care of the sheep. Now he depends on his family.

Guam disease has been attributed to the toxins found in which of the following groups?
Group of answer choices

ferns

pine trees

cycads

palm treesShare Prompt
I'm interested in their sweaters, particularly the ones made from recycled cashmere. Do they have any cashmere sweaters on sale?

00:00:07,533 --> 00:00:09,599
I found that nothing in life

now I'm sure in your experiences in school

and applying to college and

16

17
00:00:43,866 --> 00:00:45,666

18
00:00:46,866 --> 00:00:48,333

19

20

21
00:00:54,366 --> 00:00:55,533

22
00:00:55,533 --> 00:00:57,133

23

without consistency

24
00:01:01,300 --> 00:01:02,566
you'll never finish

25
00:01:04,500 --> 00:01:07,666
so do what you fee …[truncated]

I'd like you to help me develop a simple browser based game using python and flask and the javascript library d3.

The game involves controlling a swarm of von Neumann probes that try to colonize a galaxy, which is visualized as a large array of white dots on a black screen. Each von neumann probe is a small green square.

Each probe has options on what it can do: explore, reproduce, collect resources, or upgrade. Users use three slider bars to determine how much of their von neumann probes to allocate into each activity. 

Please write out the software to produce this game.

I'm thinking of making some amigurumi toys for my niece and nephew, and I need some help with sizing. Can you give me a general idea of what size hook and yarn weight I should use for a small to medium-sized toy?
What about the yarn I already have? Can I use some of my worsted weight yarn for the amigurumi toys? I have a stash of 17 skeins that I found recently, and I'd love to use them up.

I've already made a pair of fingerless gloves for my sister's birthday, and I'm thinking of making a few more as gifts. I used some of my worsted weight yarn stash, and I was wondering if I could use the same yarn and hook size for the other gloves, or if I should adjust them for different hand sizes.

I think I'll make a few more fingerless gloves using the same yarn and hook size, but I'll adjust the stitch count to fit different hand sizes. I also want to make some amigurumi toys using the same worsted weight yarn. Do you think I can use the same hook size for both projects, or should I switch to a smaller hook for the amigurumi toys?
I'm thinking of volunteering at a local park cleanup event. Do you know how I can find out about upcoming park cleanup events in my area?
Can you provide any statistics on how many people are negatively impacted by social media use?
I was thinking of making a few fingerless gloves for my friends as gifts, and I was wondering if you could give me some advice on how to size them properly.

I think I'll start with a 4.5mm hook for the amigurumi toys and adjust as needed. I also want to make some tote bags using my sewing machine, which I recently fixed. Do you have any tips for working with cotton prints, and how can I ensure that my seams are straight and professional-looking?
